commentary_text: Siraj to Jaiswal, 1 runback of a length, shaping into the pads. Jaiswal tucks it through square leg and gets off the mark Previous NextPhotos
off the mark with a boundary yet again! Full and drifting onto the pads. Sooryavanshi uses the swing and the pace and glances it into the long leg fence!
back of a length on middle and leg. Nips in sharply and Sooryavanshi gets jammed on the pad. Late on the flick
more runs! Short and down leg again. Sooryavanshi lets the ball come to him before swiping it into the long leg fence!
Siraj to Sooryavanshi, 1 runfuller outside off and this shapes in a touch too. Sooryavanshi comes forward and drives it to long on
hard length just outside off. Not a lot of room to work with and Jaiswal jabs it towards cover
Rabada to Sooryavanshi, 1 runback of a length outside off. Sooryavanshi opens his bat face and runs it down to deep third
Rabada to Jaiswal, 1 runback of a length on middle and leg. Jaiswal lets the ball come to him before glancing it down to long leg
back of a length outside off. Sooryavanshi gets up on his toes and defends
full and speared down leg. Sooryavanshi misses his flick and the umpire has no qualms in calling it a wide
very full just outside off. Sooryavanshi jams down on it and digs it out to mid on
up and over! Length just outside off and that is all Sooryavanshi needs. He sets up a solid base and muscles it over mid off!
back of a length on middle and leg. Sooryavanshi meets it right under his eyes and with relatively soft hands
Siraj to Jaiswal, SIX runsJaiswal lands his first punch now! Length ball hung up wide outside off. Jaiswal frees his arms and carves it over cover!
four more! Siraj loses his line again and pays the price. Back of a length on the pads and Jaiswal tickles it past short fine leg!
line goes awry again. On a back of a length and down leg. Jaiswal does not get bat on it and the umpire has to indulge in some more exercise
hard length on leg, tucking up the batter for room. Jaiswal hops around his crease and stabs it back to the bowler

a bit of a shank but it still carries well over mid off! Hard length outside off. Jaiswal opens up his stance and swings for the hills. Slices it a touch but still plenty on it. One bounce and into the fence!

did that carry?! And was it off the glove? Shortish and sneaking down leg. Jaiswal tries to help it fine and seems to get a nick. Buttler dives low to his right but cannot make it stick. That carried, meaning it will go down as a chance, albeit a tough one!
back of a length on middle and off. Jaiswal taps it to mid wicket and wants the single but gets sent back by his partner. Right call too
low full toss outside off. Sooryavanshi has a jab at it and chunks it off the inside half of the bat towards mid wicket
banged in short outside off and this sails over the batter's head. Easy decision for the umpire once the batter lets it go
over the top again! Width offered this time and Sooryavanshi can throw his hands at it, with this also being on the shorter side. Flayed over point!

Rabada to Sooryavanshi, 1 rundirect hit and it would have been curtains for the teenager! Zipped in full on the stumps. Sooryavanshi tips it to mid off and sets off for the run. Rashid takes aim and lets the RR opener off the hook!
back of a length outside off and this skids through. Jaiswal wants to dab this behind point but is late on the stroke
off-pace on a back of a length just outside off. Jaiswal waits for it before tapping it towards backward point
Rabada to Jaiswal, SIX runsfetch that, says Jaiswal! Banged half-way down and this sits up nicely on middle and off. Jaiswal picks it up and dumps it over the deep mid wicket fence!

nicked and grassed! Length ball slanted across the batter outside off. Sooryavanshi throws the plumbing at it and gets a healthy nick that Buttler, flying to his left, cannot quite hold on to. Another tough chance. The ball then deflects off his glove and into the deep third fence. Siraj, who was giving chase, has done some damage to himself too. GT will hope he is fine!
header: Ashok Sharma to Sooryavanshi, FOUR runs

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Sooryavanshi, SIX runsoh goodness me - that is some shot! Dug in short outside off. Sooryavanshi arches his back, uses the pace and glides it over the deep third fence!
header: Ashok Sharma to Sooryavanshi,  SIX runs

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Sooryavanshi, 1 runback of a length on off. Sooryavanshi pushes it to mid off and this time, the single is a much more comfortable exercise

oh! Sharp short ball on middle and leg. Zips through and Jaiswal, trying to upper-cut it, is late on the stroke. Buttler thinks there is bat involved but no support from elsewhere. UltraEdge returns a flat line
header: Ashok Sharma to Jaiswal, no run

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Jaiswal, 2 runsanother short ball, getting up near the ribs on off. Jaiswal opens up his stance and tugs it past mid wicket for a couple of runs
full outside off. Jaiswal comes forward and jabs it towards extra cover
header: Ashok Sharma to Jaiswal, no run

commentary_text: Prasidh to Sooryavanshi, 1 runon a length outside off. Sooryavanshi comes onto the front foot and punches it on the up into the cover region
shortish outside off. Jaiswal tries to hit it too hard and scuffs it towards extra cover
Prasidh to Jaiswal, SIX runsright out of the screws! Marginally short outside off and that is all Jaiswal needs. He stands tall and whacks it over mid on!
what timing! Back of a length on middle and off. Jaiswal gets up on his toes and clips it gently between square leg and short fine. The ball keeps gathering pace and hits the fence!
Prasidh to Jaiswal, 1 runfull toss on middle and off. Jaiswal happy to knock it to long on
back of a length on middle and leg. Sooryavanshi plays with the angle towards mid wicket
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 1 runlength outside off. Jaiswal gets onto the front foot and drives with an open bat face towards deep point

miscued and Sooryavanshi is outta here! Rashid with the breakthrough! Back of a length on middle and leg, and this seems to just stick in the surface. Sooryavanshi has a slog at it but does not get it well enough. Picks out Phillips to perfection at deep mid wicket, and the Kiwi was not dropping that - not in a million years. GT with a vital, vital wicket!Vaibhav Sooryavanshi c Phillips b Rashid Khan 31 (18b 5x4 1x6 35m) SR: 172.22 Previous NextPhotos
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runfull outside off. Jurel comes onto the front foot and knocks it to long on
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 1 runfizzed through on a length just outside off. Jaiswal pokes it towards deep square leg
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runlength ball on leg. Jurel goes back and tucks it off his pads towards deep backward square leg
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 1 runlength ball on the pads. Jaiswal stays back and works it towards deep mid wicket
very full outside off. Jaiswal jams down on it and pokes it out towards mid off
shortish on middle and off. Jaiswal hops around his crease before prodding it back to the bowler
Prasidh to Jaiswal, 1 runexcellent yorker on off. Jaiswal gets his bat down just in time and chunks it off the inside edge towards the keeper
dug in short on off. Jurel hops up onto his toes before turning it towards mid wicket
Prasidh to Jurel, 1 runback of a length outside off. Jurel uses the pace and runs it down to deep third
shot! Angled in full on leg, designed to deny room. Jaiswal, though, still manufactures some. He backs away and knifes it to backward point's left!
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runslider on a back of a length on middle. Jurel hops back and hurriedly hacks it into the on side
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 1 runlength on off. Jaiswal opens up his stance a little and punches it down to long on
oh dear - bonus runs! Speared in on a length but down leg. Jurel misses his sweep and that blinds the keeper too, who cannot come around in time!
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runon the shorter side on middle and leg. Jurel goes back and tugs it towards deep mid wicket
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 1 rungiven a bit more air, on a length outside off. Pushed down the ground to long off
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runlength on middle and off. Jurel happy to stay back and work it to deep mid wicket
Rashid Khan to Jaiswal, 2 runsshortish on middle and leg. Jaiswal rocks back and whips it between deep mid wicket and long on. Good work by Ashok to his left to keep it to two
fullish outside off. Jurel gets onto the front foot and stabs it to mid off
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: smashed! On a length wide outside off. Bowled at 145.7kph but that probably went off the bat quicker. Jurel throws his hands at it and belts it over extra cover!
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, FOUR runs

commentary_text: back of a length just outside off. Jurel gets up on his toes and defends
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: slanted in full on the pads. Jurel flicks it straight to mid wicket
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, 1 runslower ball on a back of a length outside off. Jurel hacks it towards deep square leg
banged in short and tucking up the batter on leg. Jaiswal plays all over it and gets pinged on the body
header: Ashok Sharma to Jaiswal, no run

commentary_text: top placement! A touch short on the stumps and Jurel makes the most ot it. He gets his front hip out of the way and punches it crisply past deep mid wicket!
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel, FOUR runs

commentary_text: Rashid Khan to Jurel, 2 runsgoogly on a length on off. Jurel reads it off the pitch and turns it between deep mid wicket and deep backward square leg. Back for a brace

Buttler and Rashid go up for a caught-behind, but umpire Rohan Pandit says no. GT, after some deliberation, review! Darted in full and down leg. Jurel looks for the sweep and seems to play all over it. And replays confirm that suspicion. Clear gap between the bat/gloves and the ball, and GT burn a review!
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: length ball hung up wide outside off. Jurel tries the reverse this time but only swishes at thin air
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: shortish just outside off. Jurel goes back and stabs it to mid wicket
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: streaky but Jurel will not mind it one bit! Back of a length outside off. Kicks up a little off the surface and Jurel, wafting at it, gets a thick outside edge that flies past the keeper!
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel, FOUR runs

commentary_text: back of a length outside off. Jaiswal looks for the cut but is beaten for pace as the ball skids through after pitching
class stamped all over that, and Jaiswal brings up a half-century! Back of a length outside off. Jaiswal rises with the bounce and threads it between extra cover and mid off!

well well! Zipped in full on middle and off. Jaiswal moves across his stumps. trying to scoop but ends up chalking it off the bottom of the bat into the off side. Jaiswal, for his troubles, falls to the deck and has to then scurry to complete the single
very full on middle and leg. Jurel shuffles across before flicking it straight to mid wicket
not entirely in control, but Jurel will take it! Slower bumper outside off. Jurel has a swipe at it and the ball loops off the top edge over the keeper!
Siraj to Jurel, SIX runsJurel goes big now! Siraj struggling with his lines and lengths. This is a full toss on the pads and Jurel shovels it over deep square leg!
safe, just! Hard length just outside off. Jaiswal has a half-hearted stab at it and nearly pops it to mid off. On the bounce eventually
brilliant batting! Back of a length delivery wide outside off. Jaiswal telegraphs the line, moves across and laps it past the keeper!

off stump thumped, and Rabada has the last laugh! On a length and zoning into off stump. Jaiswal backs away, with designs to go inside-out. Comfortably late on the stroke, though, and the ball whistles past the bat before leaving off stump in a mess. Another tone-setting knock by Jaiswal comes to an end!Yashasvi Jaiswal b Rabada 55 (36b 6x4 3x6 61m) SR: 152.77
Rabada to Parag, SIX runsoh, hello! Has Parag been batting in the nets all along?! Length ball sliding into the pads. Parag snaps his wrists into action and whips it over deep square leg!
Rabada to Parag, 2 runsback of a length outside off. Parag uses the pace and dabs it past backward point for a couple of runs
banged into the track on middle and leg. Parag gets up on his toes and fends it into the on side
Prasidh to Jurel, 1 runsafe again! Full on middle and off. Jurel plants his front foot and then just chips it without much conviction. Falls short of long on, thankfully for him

catching practice this time, and RR losing their way a little now! Seems like a change-up, on a length outside off. Parag does not quite pick it, and the ball stops in the surface too, further compounding the RR skipper's woes. Plonks it towards backward point, where Kushagra does just enough to cling on. Parag disappointed; GT have a foothold!Riyan Parag c Kushagra b Prasidh Krishna 8 (4b 0x4 1x6 4m) SR: 200
fullish just outside off. Hetmyer comes forward and defends

appeal for a caught-behind, but the umpire responds with a wide call! Sharp short ball that tails down leg. Hetmyer is late on the pull and seems to miss it. Keeper, bowler and Phillips think this has grazed the glove on the way but not enough conviction to take it upstairs. It does look very close, though, and UltraEdge confirms there was a murmur off the glove!
low full toss outside off. Hetmyer prods it out towards extra cover
Prasidh to Hetmyer, SIX runsinto the stands! Banged in short on middle and leg, and Hetmyer picks this up really early. He swivels and helps it over long leg!
Prasidh to Hetmyer, SIX runsthis is incredible hitting! Back of a length, sliding into middle and leg. Hetmyer jumps inside the line, picks it up and deposits it over deep backward square leg!
darted in on a length but also down leg. Jurel does not get bat on it and the umpire stretches out his arms
Rashid Khan to Jurel, SIX runsJurel clicking into over-drive now! Given a hint of air on off, and Jurel is down the track in a trice. Picks the googly too and slugs it over wide long on!
header: Rashid Khan to Jurel,  SIX runs

commentary_text: Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runshortish on middle and leg. Jurel goes back and works it towards deep backward square leg
Rashid Khan to Hetmyer, 1 runlength ball, spinning into the pads. Hetmyer tucks it towards deep backward square leg
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runback of a length outside off. Skids through and Jurel jams it into the pitch, with the ball bouncing up over Rashid
Rashid Khan to Hetmyer, 1 runtossed up a touch on a fullish length on middle and off. Hetmyer prods it enough to the bowler's right and hares across to the other end
Rashid Khan to Jurel, 1 runshort and wide outside off. Jurel reaches out for it and cuts it towards deep point
Ashok Sharma to Jurel, 1 runback of a length outside off. Jurel uses the pace and runs it down to deep third
shot! Back of a length just outside off. Hetmyer plants his front foot and with a wave of the willow, pings it over mid wicket!
header: Ashok Sharma to Hetmyer, FOUR runs

commentary_text: flat and straight to Siraj at long on! Ashok with another timely wicket! Full outside off and Hetmyer's eyes light up. He clears his front leg and swings hard at it. Gets a good chunk of it but not enough elevation. Siraj shimmies to his left and gobbles up the chance smartly to his left. GT keep chipping away!Shimron Hetmyer c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma 18 (8b 1x4 2x6 14m) SR: 225 Previous NextPhotos
into the blockhole on middle and off. Ferreira jams down on it and clinks it out towards the bowler
header: Ashok Sharma to Ferreira, no run

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Ferreira, 1 runvery full again, zoning into the stumps. Ferreira scuffs it towards mid off, where Siraj fumbles, allowing the new batter to open his account
searing yorker on middle and off. Jurel jams it off the inside edge into his pads. That was bowled at almost 154kph. Another brilliant over by Ashok!
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, no run

commentary_text: skewed up, and Rabada gets his fellow South African! Dug in short outside off and this seems to get a tad big on the batter. Ferreira swipes at it because, well, why not, but gets it all wrong. Simple enough catch for Phillips at deep mid wicket, and RR are now five down!Donovan Ferreira c Phillips b Rabada 1 (3b 0x4 0x6 6m) SR: 33.33
Rabada to Jadeja, 1 runon a length outside off. Jadeja has a tentative stab at it and chunks it off the inside half of the bat towards deep square leg

Rabada to Jurel, SIX runsanother miscue, but this has just enough sauce on it! Speared in full on middle and leg. Jurel's bat face opens upon contact, and the ball goes a mile up. Siraj takes it at long on and then realizes he will step over. He tries to parry it but that parry does not allow him to get back into the field of play and effect the catch. Loose palm in the end, and Jurel gets six!
banged in much shorter outside off. Jurel looks to upper-cut it but fails to lay bat on ball
Rabada to Jurel, 2 runsa 30-ball fifty for Jurel! Angled in full on middle and leg. Jurel shuffles across and flicks it through mid wicket. Sharp work by Sudharsan on the fence to keep it to two
Rabada to Jurel, 1 rundarted in full on the pads. Jurel works it towards deep mid wicket and has to settle for a single
fullish just outside off. Jadeja looks to go inside-out but only connects with the fresh Ahmedabad air
Ashok Sharma to Jurel, SIX runsfetch that too, says Jurel! Angled in on a length on off. Jurel picks it up very early and wallops it over deep mid wicket!
header: Ashok Sharma to Jurel,  SIX runs

commentary_text: Ashok Sharma to Jurel, 1 runslower ball on a back of a length outside off. Jurel backs away and jabs it towards deep point
Ashok Sharma to Jadeja, 1 runshort and wide outside off. Jadeja reaches out for it and cuts it towards deep point
slower ball hung up full outside off. Too wide, though, and called as such once Jurel lets it go
Ashok Sharma to Jurel, 2 runshard length, honing into off. Hits the bat hard but Jurel still manages to bunt it to deep mid wicket for a brace
Ashok Sharma to Jurel, 1 runslower ball on a back of a length outside off. Jurel dabs it to short third and stays content with the run
slower ball but the line goes awry again. Down leg and some more exercise for the umpire
back of a length outside off and this skips through. Jadeja has a waft at it and sees the ball whistle past his flailing blade
header: Ashok Sharma to Jadeja, no run

commentary_text: Prasidh to Jurel, 2 runsvery full on middle and leg. Jurel rolls his wrists and eases it between deep mid wicket and deep square leg. Back for another brace
Prasidh to Jurel, 1 runfull toss zoning into off. Jurel tries to hit it too hard and drags it towards deep mid wicket
Prasidh to Jadeja, 1 runlow full toss, spearing into leg. Jadeja backs away and tugs it towards deep square leg

Prasidh to Jurel, SIX runsup, up and away! Length ball on leg and that is just what Jurel wants. He plants his front foot and clubs it just over deep mid wicket. Plenty of height and enough distance, as it turns out!
Prasidh to Jurel, 1 runinto the blockhole outside off. Jurel squeezes it out towards extra cover
slanted into the stumps. Jadeja has a massive swing at it and seems to chunk it off the inside edge into his back leg
done with surgical precision! Very full on off but this is not quite a yorker. Jurel lets his wrists power through and muscles it between long on and deep mid wicket!
short and wide outside off. Jurel jumps up onto his toes and wafts at it, only to connect with fresh air

banged in much shorter outside off, and Jurel nicks it through! Looks for the same stroke, trying to upper-cut it over the bowler. This, though, has just enough kick on it to make the shot untenable. A bit of a variation too and that lack of pace means Jurel only finds Buttler with his effort. A brilliant knock but someone else will need to take RR close to 210 now!Dhruv Jurel c †Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 75 (42b 5x4 5x6 71m) SR: 178.57
excellent yorker on off. Archer gets his bat down in time but cannot do much with it. Plays it back to the bowler
Siraj to Archer, 1 runanother yorker on off. Archer has a pop at it and squirts it off the inside edge towards deep backward square leg. Jadeja back on strike

Jadeja finishes the innings with a boundary, and RR have made their way to 210! Juicy full toss just outside off. Jadeja gets his front foot out of the way and smears it into the deep square leg fence. Some momentum to take into the next innings!
